name: Joe Sullivan
abstract: Michael Joseph O'Sullivan (November 4, 1906 – October 13, 1971) was an American jazz pianist. Sullivan was the ninth child of Irish immigrant parents.

about_quotes.speaker: Wild Bill Davison
about_quotes.evidence: I had taken Bobby Hackett's place with The Joe Sullivan Trio -- with Hayes Alvis on the base -- and Joe Sullivan and me.

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Carter
about_quotes.evidence: The transcript quotes Mezzrow's book stating Joe Sullivan agreed to join a band, but the interviewer explicitly notes 'you may not have been around' and the interviewee does not confirm personal participation or acquaintance.

about_quotes.speaker: Johnny Varro
about_quotes.evidence: I’d watch Joe Sullivan and Gene Schroeder and all © Fillius Jazz Archive -5- the piano players that played... And I got a great education just listening to these guys play
about_quotes.doc_title: Johnny Varro interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 16, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: I think the usual suspects. Joe Sullivan. Pee Wee Russell. Just an absolute group of alcoholics... They were all such good musicians.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Wilber
about_quotes.evidence: this was before the generation of Joe Sullivan and Jess Stacey, who were emulating Earl Hines and Fats and people like that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Wilber interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, May 22, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Davern
about_quotes.evidence: That influenced me was Pee Wee Russell playing “The Last Time I Saw Chicago,” with Joe Sullivan and Zutty Singleton.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Davern interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 16, 2001 [interview 2 of 2]
